page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.ic5.4xlarge is an x86_64 architecture server instance powered by an Intel Xeon 8163 processor running at 2.5 GHz. It features 16 dedicated physical cores with 1 thread per core, providing 16 vCPUs. The instance is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, resulting in a low memory-to-core ratio of 1.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, and operates on a KVM hypervisor with a baseline network bandwidth of 5 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates a clear distinction between single-core and multi-core capabilities. Single-core performance is weak, as shown by stress-ng and Geekbench single-core scores falling in the bottom 25%, and single-threaded gzip compression ranking in the bottom 10%. However, multi-core performance scales to average levels, with Passmark CPU, Geekbench multi-core, and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression all registering in the middle 50%. Memory bandwidth tests show average read and write speeds, though the Passmark memory score remains in the bottom 25%.

The primary tradeoff of the ecs.ic5.4xlarge is its highly asymmetric resource allocation, offering substantial compute cores but very limited memory capacity. This makes it a cost-efficient option for parallelizable workloads that require multi-core processing without high memory overhead. It is suitable for multi-threaded batch processing, static web serving, and basic database operations, but is poorly suited for memory-intensive applications, single-threaded tasks, or large-scale language model inference.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.ic5.4xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized server instance featuring 16 dedicated Intel Xeon 8163 vCPUs running at 2.5 GHz and 16.0 GB of RAM. This configuration results in a lean 1.0 GB of memory per core. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, relying on a KVM hypervisor and offering a baseline network bandwidth of 5 Gbps. Performance benchmarks reveal a significant contrast: single-core CPU performance and single-threaded compression speeds are weak, while multi-core performance scales to average levels. This resource profile makes the instance highly cost-efficient for parallelized, compute-bound workloads that do not require large memory footprints. It is best suited for multi-threaded batch processing, static web serving, and lightweight database operations, whereas memory-heavy or single-threaded applications will face performance bottlenecks.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.ic5.4xlarge is an x86_64 server with 16 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 16 GB RAM. It offers average multi-core performance but limited memory per core and weak single-core speeds.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.ic5.4xlarge features 16 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 16 GB RAM, offering average multi-core performance for compute-bound tasks.
tagline: A compute-focused, memory-lean virtual server optimized for parallelized multi-core workloads and static web serving without high memory overhead.

No local storage or GPU acceleration included.
